image.alt: Ornate painted portrait of an English queen in an elaborate jeweled gown

rounds.revealFact: Elizabeth I ruled England for 44 years, a period later called the Elizabethan era.

image.alt: Painting of massed cavalry and infantry clashing on a smoke-filled battlefield

attribution.fileTitle: File:Thomas Jones Barker, The Battle of Waterloo.jpg
attribution.author: Thomas Jones Barker

clues: An exiled emperor's final campaign ended when a late-arriving army tipped the balance against him.
Muddy ground from overnight rain delayed the attack and may have changed the outcome.

events.label: Michelangelo begins painting the Sistine Chapel ceiling
Isaac Newton is born in Woolsthorpe, England
James Watt patents a greatly improved steam engine
Nicéphore Niépce captures the first permanent photograph

followUpQuestion.prompt: Where did this flight take place?

rounds.prompt: Mount Vesuvius erupts, burying the Roman city of Pompeii in ash.
rounds.revealFact: The eruption preserved much of Pompeii almost perfectly, giving historians a detailed snapshot of Roman daily life.

rounds.prompt: Where in the world is the city of Timbuktu?
rounds.revealFact: A Saharan trading and scholarly hub of the Mali Empire, Timbuktu was famed for its medieval manuscripts and universities.
